qa_pairs.question: What are the consequences if a faculty member with sponsored users allows their Alliance account to expire?
qa_pairs.answer: If a faculty member with sponsored users allows their account to expire, both their account and the accounts of all sponsored users will be deactivated. Any submitted jobs will be paused, and the research group will lose access to their files until the sponsor account is reactivated.

qa_pairs.question: What action should be taken if a faculty member determines that their Alliance account or the accounts of their sponsored users are no longer required?
qa_pairs.answer: If a faculty member's account or those of their sponsored users are no longer needed, they can simply let them expire.

qa_pairs.question: What steps are involved for a faculty member to renew their Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: To renew their Alliance account, a faculty member will receive an email during the renewal period with a link to the CCDB form. They must update their contact information and Field of Research, complete a User Satisfaction Survey, and update the status of their sponsored roles. Finally, they need to click the 'REQUEST ACCOUNT RENEWAL' button.

qa_pairs.question: What specific update is required from university faculty regarding their sponsored users during the account renewal process?
qa_pairs.answer: University faculty who sponsor other users must update the status of these accounts, indicating whether the sponsored users are continuing or if their work is done and the sponsorship has ended.

qa_pairs.question: If a Principal Investigator (PI) with a faculty role moves to a different Canadian academic institution, will users sponsored under their old role be automatically transferred to their new role?
qa_pairs.answer: No, if a PI with a faculty role moves to a different institution, users sponsored with their old role will not be automatically transferred. Any user the PI wants to sponsor with their new faculty role will have to apply for a new role sponsored by the new faculty position.

qa_pairs.question: What happens to a sponsored user's access to a Resource Allocation Project (RAP) if their PI moves institutions and they don't apply for a new sponsored role?
qa_pairs.answer: If a PI moves institutions, users sponsored with their old role who have a RAC allocation will lose access to the Resource Allocation Project (RAP) where their resources are allocated.

qa_pairs.question: How can a Principal Investigator (PI) manage members of their Resource Allocation Project (RAP)?
qa_pairs.answer: A PI can add to and remove RAP members by navigating to 'My Projects' and then 'Manage Project Membership' on CCDB.

qa_pairs.question: Can a Principal Investigator (PI) access the files of a sponsored user after indicating that the user has moved on and will no longer continue under their sponsorship?
qa_pairs.answer: Consent from that user may be required for the PI to access the files. It is important to make arrangements with the sponsored user as soon as possible to prevent them from becoming incommunicado. For more information, consult the 'Access to files of users leaving a project' page.

qa_pairs.question: What are the renewal requirements for users with a sponsored role, such as a Doctoral, Masters, or External Collaborator?
qa_pairs.answer: Users with a sponsored role will receive an email with a link to the account renewal form. They must update their contact information, complete the form, fill out a short User Satisfaction Survey, and then click the 'REQUEST ACCOUNT RENEWAL' button to initiate the request.

qa_pairs.question: When will a sponsored account be activated after the sponsored user has completed their renewal request?
qa_pairs.answer: Once a sponsored user has completed their renewal request, their account will be renewed immediately or as soon as their sponsor renews their own account.

qa_pairs.question: What should a sponsored user do if their sponsor indicates that they have moved on, graduated, or changed positions?
qa_pairs.answer: If a sponsor indicates that a sponsored user has moved on, graduated, or changed positions, the user will not be able to renew that particular role. Instead, they must apply for a new role that accurately reflects their current circumstances (e.g., new sponsor, institution, or position).

qa_pairs.question: My sponsored role is still deactivated even after I completed the renewal on CCDB. What could be the reason?
qa_pairs.answer: If your sponsored role is still deactivated after you've completed its renewal on CCDB, it is likely because your sponsor has not yet renewed or reactivated their own account. Your role should be activated once your sponsor renews their account and confirms your role.
